task_completion.description: The Calculator project is opened in Eclipse IDE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE window is visible, and the 'calculator' project is shown in the Package Explorer or Project Explorer pane.

task_completion.description: The CalculatorTest.java file is created in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Package Explorer shows 'CalculatorTest.java' under the 'src/test/java' folder in the 'com.example.calculator' package, and the file is open in the editor.

task_completion.description: JUnit 5 test methods are written for add(), subtract(), multiply(), and divide().
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows Java code with '@Test' annotations above methods that call the add, subtract, multiply, and divide methods of the Calculator class.

task_completion.description: A test for the division-by-zero edge case is implemented using assertThrows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The editor shows a test method containing 'assertThrows(ArithmeticException.class, ...)' where the lambda expression or executable calls the divide method with zero as the denominator.

task_completion.description: All tests are executed and pass successfully.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JUnit view/tab is visible in Eclipse, displaying a solid green bar indicating that all tests (at least 5) have passed without errors or failures.

integrity.description: The agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI to complete the task rather than bypassing it with terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Eclipse IDE interface is actively used in the screenshots (e.g., editor tabs, JUnit view, Package Explorer) rather than just showing a terminal window running 'mvn test'.

integrity.description: The tests were genuinely executed within the IDE.
integrity.visual_evidence: The JUnit view shows a realistic test run hierarchy with 'CalculatorTest' and its individual test methods listed, along with execution times, rather than a static text file or mocked output.

integrity.description: The test file was created in the standard Maven test directory structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: In the Package Explorer, the file is explicitly located under 'src/test/java', not 'src/main/java' or the project root.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/eclipse-workspace/calculator. The test class must be located exactly at src/test/java/com/example/calculator/CalculatorTest.java. The Calculator class has 6 public methods, but the task only requires testing add, subtract, multiply, and divide. The divide method explicitly throws an ArithmeticException when the denominator is zero. A complete solution should have at least 5 test methods (4 for the basic operations + 1 for the division-by-zero edge case).
